name: Battleship Missouri Memorial
tagline: Stand on the exact deck where WWII ended — the Mighty Mo pairs perfectly with the Arizona Memorial across the harbor.
parking: Use the Pearl Harbor Visitor Center lot ($7/day). Shuttle bus takes you to Ford Island
hours: Daily 8am–4pm. Closed Thanksgiving, Christmas, New Year's Day
bestTime: Morning — do the Arizona Memorial first (free, timed tickets), then shuttle to the Missouri. Plan 1.5–2 hours aboard
kamaaina: Kamaaina/military rates available with valid ID
talkStory: On the surrender deck, two copies of the instrument of surrender were signed — one leather-bound for the Allies, one canvas-covered for Japan. Captain Callaghan ordered the kamikaze pilot who struck the starboard side in 1945 be given a proper burial at sea with military honors, a gesture of respect still recounted by tour guides. The Missouri was deliberately positioned bow-to-bow with the sunken Arizona, symbolically bookending WWII. Ask about the 'Heart of the Missouri' engine room tour that most visitors skip.
